title: Fix 5 scheduler followup tests broken by reactive slot-fill
description:
The reactive slot-fill loop (B-0d69af81) now dispatches corrective beads in the same run_once() call as the bead that triggered their creation. Five tests in tests/test_scheduler_followups.py are broken because they assert result.blocked == [original_bead_id] but the corrective bead now also runs (and blocks with FakeRunner having no configured result) in the same cycle, so it also appears in result.blocked.

Failing tests:
- test_corrective_completion_requeues_blocked_review_parent_immediately
- test_corrective_completion_requeues_blocked_tester_parent_immediately
- test_developer_needs_changes_does_not_create_immediate_corrective
- test_review_needs_changes_creates_corrective_immediately
- test_tester_needs_changes_creates_corrective_immediately

The fix: in each failing test, update assertions from strict equality (assertEqual([bead_id], result.blocked)) to assertIn(bead_id, result.blocked) where needed, OR configure FakeRunner with an explicit result for the corrective bead to prevent it from blocking unexpectedly. Do NOT change the core scheduler behavior — only the test assertions and/or FakeRunner setup to match the new reactive behavior.

Run the 5 tests to confirm they pass after the fix. Do not change any non-test source files.
